name: Penya Foradà Loop from La Carroja
name_original: La Penya Foradà — Circular desde La Carroja
description: Hike the moderate Penya Foradà Loop from La Carroja, a 6.1-mile route with stunning valley views and a unique rock arch.
region_intro: Hiking around la Vall de Gallinera offers exploration through a landscape characterized by lush mountains and valleys. The region features dramatic natural formations like the Penya Foradà, a striking natural stone arch, and is embraced by the Sierra de Foradada. Trails often traverse fragrant pine forests and, in spring, extensive cherry orchards, with numerous springs emerging from steep rock faces. Best hiking trails around la Vall de Gallinera The most popular hiking route is Penya Foradà Loop from La Carroja, a 6.1 miles (9.9 km) trail that takes 3 hours 27 minutes to complete. This moderate route leads to the iconic Penya Foradà. Another top favourite among local hikers is The Route of the 8 Villages through the Vall de la Gallinera, a difficult 16.3 miles (26.3 km) path. This extensive trail links the eight picturesque villages of the valley, often following agricultural roads and connecting various springs. Local hikers also love the Old Washhouse – Route of the 8 Villages loop from la Vall de Gallinera, a 3.9 miles (6.3 km) trail leading through varied terrain, often completed in about 1 hour 46 minutes. Hiking around la Vall de Gallinera is defined by lush mountains, pine forests, and historical village connections. The network offers options for different ability levels, from easy loops to more challenging long-distance routes. The routes in la Vall de Gallinera are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.5 stars from nearly 400 reviews. More than 2,100 hikers have used komoot to explore la Vall de Gallinera's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many hiking trails are available in La Vall de Gallinera?
A: La Vall de Gallinera boasts a diverse network of over 35 hiking trails. These routes range from easy strolls to challenging long-distance paths, ensuring options for every experience level.

Q: What kind of natural features can I expect to see while hiking in La Vall de Gallinera?
A: Hikers in La Vall de Gallinera will encounter a dramatic landscape of lush mountains and valleys, embraced by the Sierra de Foradada. A prominent natural wonder is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2885240" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Penya Foradà&lt;/a&gt;, a striking natural stone arch. You'll also traverse fragrant pine forests, extensive cherry orchards (especially beautiful in spring), and numerous pristine springs emerging from the rock faces.

Q: Are there any easy or family-friendly hiking routes in the area?
A: Yes, La Vall de Gallinera offers 7 easy hiking routes suitable for beginners or families. An example is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/14679207" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Old Washhouse – Route of the 8 Villages loop from la Vall de Gallinera&lt;/a&gt;, which is 6.3 km long and takes about 1 hour 46 minutes to complete, offering varied terrain.

Q: What are some of the historical sites or landmarks I can explore on the trails?
A: The valley is rich in history. You can discover remnants of Moorish watchtowers, medieval castles like Benirrama Castle, and the ruins of an 18th-century convent near Benitaia. Some trails, like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/10358554" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Alpatrò Trail – Castell de Benissili loop&lt;/a&gt;, offer opportunities to explore significant historical sites such as the Castle of Benissili and the Iberian Settlement of Xarpolar. Prehistoric cave paintings can also be found in caves near Benirrama and Benialí, such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/7510298" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Camino de Petracos&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Are there any routes that connect the picturesque villages of the valley?
A: Absolutely. The most notable is &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e871827745/the-route-of-the-8-villages-through-the-vall-de-la-gallinera" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;The Route of the 8 Villages&lt;/a&gt;, an extensive trail linking Benirrama, Benialí, Benisivá, Benitaia, La Carroja, Alpatró, Llombai, and Benisili. This route, largely following agricultural roads, allows you to experience the unique charm and historical elements of each village.

Q: What do other hikers say about the trails in La Vall de Gallinera?
A: The hiking routes in La Vall de Gallinera are highly regarded by the komoot community, with an average rating of 4.5 stars from nearly 400 reviews. Hikers often praise the lush mountains, fragrant pine forests, and the historical connections between the villages, highlighting the well-maintained network suitable for various ability levels.

Q: When is the best time to visit La Vall de Gallinera for hiking?
A: Spring is particularly beautiful due to the extensive cherry orchards blooming into a pink-hued spectacle. Additionally, a unique phenomenon occurs twice a year, in March and October, when sunlight passes directly through the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2885240" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Penya Foradà&lt;/a&gt; arch, illuminating the Calvary of Benitaia, making these times especially appealing.

Q: Are there any challenging routes for experienced hikers?
A: Yes, for experienced hikers seeking a challenge, La Vall de Gallinera offers 6 difficult routes. An example is &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e871827745/the-route-of-the-8-villages-through-the-vall-de-la-gallinera" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;The Route of the 8 Villages&lt;/a&gt;, which is 26.3 km long and involves significant elevation changes, providing a rewarding experience.

Q: Can I find circular hiking routes in La Vall de Gallinera?
A: Yes, many routes in La Vall de Gallinera are designed as loops. For instance, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e871856620/penya-forada-loop-from-la-carroja" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Penya Foradà Loop from La Carroja&lt;/a&gt; is a moderate 9.9 km trail that takes you to the iconic Penya Foradà and brings you back to your starting point.

Q: Are there any specific routes that highlight the local flora?
A: Yes, there is a specialized Botanical Route designed to highlight the local flora. Along this trail, plants are identified by informational panels and QR codes, offering an educational experience for those interested in the region's plant life.

Q: What are the best viewpoints in La Vall de Gallinera?
A: Many trails offer spectacular vistas, particularly those leading to higher elevations. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e871856620/penya-forada-loop-from-la-carroja" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Foradada Route&lt;/a&gt;, for example, provides breathtaking views of the valley and surrounding mountains, especially from the vicinity of the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2885240" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Penya Foradà&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Are there any caves or unique rock formations to visit?
A: Beyond the famous &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2885240" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Penya Foradà&lt;/a&gt;, the region features several caves. You can explore sites like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4042839" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Forat de la Juvea&lt;/a&gt; and &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3326567" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cova Obrada del Moro&lt;/a&gt;, some of which contain prehistoric cave paintings near Benirrama and Benialí.
tips:
It is an icon of the Vall de la Gallinera. Not only does it offer amazing views of the entire valley, but its curious arch or window shape attracts all who are curious and adventurous to reach it.
Formed naturally by erosion over time by wind and water, it also has the particularity that twice a year, precisely in March and October, the sun lines up with the arch and can be seen right through it, providing a matchless show.
Climbing to the top can be a challenge but the views will be more than rewarding!
An old Moorish cave that is believed to have been used both as a refuge and as a defense against the withdrawal of the Moors from the area. Today you can visit and enter it with due caution.

faqs.question: What is the difficulty level of the Penya Foradà Loop from La Carroja?
faqs.answer: This loop is considered moderate. It requires good fitness and sure-footedness, especially in certain sections. While paths are generally accessible, be mindful of potential trip hazards like stones and exercise caution near cliffs, particularly around Penya Foradà and Cova Obrada del Moro.

faqs.question: What are the main sights and viewpoints along the Penya Foradà Loop?
faqs.answer: The highlight of the route is undoubtedly the Penya Foradà itself, a striking natural arch offering incredible panoramic views of the entire Vall de la Gallinera. You'll also pass by the Cova Obrada del Moro, a historic Moorish cave, and may encounter fields of cherry trees, especially beautiful in bloom. In clear weather, you might even spot the sea from higher points.

faqs.question: When is the best time of year to hike the Penya Foradà Loop?
faqs.answer: The trail offers unique experiences in different seasons. March and October are particularly special due to the solar alignment when the sun's rays pass directly through the Penya Foradà arch. March is also ideal for seeing the cherry tree fields in full bloom. Generally, spring and autumn provide pleasant temperatures for hiking.

faqs.question: Is the Penya Foradà Loop from La Carroja dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: Yes, the Penya Foradà Loop is generally dog-friendly. However, given the moderate difficulty and sections requiring sure-footedness, ensure your dog is accustomed to hiking on varied terrain. Always keep your dog on a leash and be mindful of other hikers and wildlife.

faqs.question: Are there any permits or entrance fees required for this trail?
faqs.answer: No, there are no specific permits or entrance fees required to hike the Penya Foradà Loop from La Carroja. The trail is freely accessible to the public.

faqs.question: Where does the Penya Foradà Loop trailhead start, and is there parking available?
faqs.answer: The loop typically starts from La Carroja. Parking is usually available within or near the village, though specific designated parking areas might vary. It's advisable to arrive early, especially on weekends or during peak seasons, to secure a spot.

faqs.question: What makes the Penya Foradà arch so special?
faqs.answer: The Penya Foradà arch is a natural wonder, a window-shaped rock formation carved by erosion. Its most remarkable feature is the twice-yearly solar alignment, occurring precisely in March and October. During these times, the sun aligns perfectly with the arch, and its rays shine directly through, illuminating the spot where a Franciscan convent once stood. This natural spectacle attracts many visitors.

faqs.question: Does the trail intersect with other well-known paths?
faqs.answer: Yes, the route incorporates sections of several local paths. You'll find yourself on parts of the Camí d'Alpatró, Assegador de la Carroja, Camí de la Carroja, and Camí de la Garriga, among others, as you navigate the loop.

faqs.question: What should I bring for a hike on this trail?
faqs.answer: For this moderate hike, we recommend bringing plenty of water, snacks, appropriate hiking footwear with good grip, sun protection (hat, sunscreen), and layers of clothing suitable for changing weather conditions. A basic first-aid kit and a charged phone are also advisable.

faqs.question: Are there any amenities like cafes or accommodation near the trailhead in La Carroja?
faqs.answer: La Carroja is a small village, but you may find basic amenities. For a wider selection of cafes, pubs, or accommodation, you might need to explore other villages within the Vall de la Gallinera or nearby larger towns.

faqs.question: What kind of views can I expect along the Penya Foradà Loop?
faqs.answer: The loop offers amazing views of the entire Vall de la Gallinera, including its eight villages. From the Penya Foradà arch, the panoramas are particularly stunning. While some areas show signs of past fires, the landscape is regenerating with scrubland and pine trees, providing a mix of wild and scenic vistas.
